title: TPCH Suburb Research Output Schema
description: Strict schema for the JSON payload produced by the Suburb Research Agent (the local .claude/skills/suburb-research skill, and any future research-agent edge function). Any field written into the suburb_research table must conform to this shape. Keep this file aligned with supabase/functions/upload-research/prompt.ts. v2.0.0 retired the original 12 EXTENDED pillars; the report now uses 12 CORE pillars only, with comparable_sales as the only promoted top-level field. cashflow_model was retired in April 2026 as cash-flow modelling belongs at project level, not suburb level.

schema_version.description: Must equal SCHEMA_VERSION in supabase/functions/upload-research/prompt.ts. Bump together when the JSON shape changes. v2.1.0 added narrative_thesis. v2.2.0 enforced thesis_main as a rich 3-4 paragraph Executive Summary (min 1200 chars), distinct from the long-form narrative_thesis.

suburb.description: Suburb name in title case, e.g. 'Southbank'.

region.description: Free-text region label until region_research is built (Phase 2). E.g. 'Inner Melbourne'.

lga.description: Local Government Area, e.g. 'City of Melbourne'.

conviction_score.description: MUST equal the exact sum of the 5 dimension scores.

rating.description: Must match the band implied by conviction_score. 80-100 Strong Buy, 60-79 Good Buy, 40-59 Watch, 0-39 Caution.

thesis_short.description: One-line summary used on suburb cards. No em-dashes, no hype.

thesis_main.description: Rich Executive Summary, 3-4 paragraphs, approximately 2,500 to 3,000 chars. Structure: para 1 cycle position + cross-city comparables; para 2 leading indicators that contradict the trailing print (vacancy, rent, yield); para 3 supply math (demand-per-year, in-train pipeline, absorption window, statewide shortfall, land-constraint); para 4 catalysts already live + closing rating sentence. Every numerical claim cited inline (Source: Publisher, Date). Distinct from narrative_thesis (which is the voice-led Plain English Story version).

narrative_thesis.description: Long-form 'The [suburb] story, in plain English' narrative. Six-part framework: cycle position; cross-city comparables; the contrarian signal; tenant pool; catalysts; shape of the opportunity. Includes a dedicated 'Supply versus demand' subsection (between catalysts and shape) carrying the full demand math + supply math + absorption math + statewide context + land-constraint assessment + balance conclusion. Every factual claim cited inline (Source: Publisher, Date).

pillars.description: All 12 pillars must be present with status:'ok'. EXTENDED pillars retired in v2.0.0.

endorsements.description: ≥3 third-party articles or research-house calls supporting the suburb thesis. Each within 12 months of today.

response.description: 1-2 sentences contextualising why the buy thesis still holds, using your own data. Not a dismissal.

comparable_sales.description: 0-10 named recent transactions. Top-level field (not a pillar) since v2.0.0. Leave empty array if not available.

sources.description: Master citation list. Every URL referenced anywhere in the report must appear here.

tag.description: Short label referenced from each pillar's citation_tags array.

hero_metrics.description: Denormalised hero strip values. Populated where data exists, null otherwise.

score_reasoning.description: 2-3 sentences. (a) Why this number, (b) what data points pushed it up or down, (c) what would need to change to move it.

pillar.description: All 12 pillars are mandatory. Status MUST be 'ok'. Headline, narrative, and stats must be populated.

narrative.description: 2-3 paragraphs. Every factual claim carries an inline (Source: Publisher, Date) citation.

chart_data.description: Structured data the UI can render as a bar/line chart, or null.

citation_tags.description: Each tag must appear in the top-level sources list.

excerpt.description: 1-3 sentences quoting or paraphrasing the supportive claim.

x-pre-flight-self-check.description: Non-standard extension. The agent should self-verify these rules before emitting the JSON. Enforcement is duplicated in the upload-research edge function validator.
rules: All 12 pillars present with status:'ok'. (EXTENDED pillars retired in v2.0.0.)
conviction_score equals the exact sum of the 5 dimension scores.
rating matches the band: 80+ Strong Buy, 60-79 Good Buy, 40-59 Watch, 0-39 Caution.
endorsements array has 3 or more items, every URL is full https://, every date within 12 months of today, all 6 required fields per item.
Exactly 1 counter_view with a populated response field of ≥30 chars.
Every citation_tag in any pillar appears in the top-level sources list.
No em-dash character (U+2014) anywhere in any string.
No banned jargon undescribed (institutional-grade, blue-chip, investment-grade, premium offering, world-class, once-in-a-generation, unmissable).
No XML or markdown citation tags (<cite>, [^1], etc.).
Every dimension score_reasoning is ≥80 chars (2-3 substantive sentences).
Narrative thesis section ('The [suburb] story, in plain English') follows the six-part framework with no forecasted numbers and all claims cited inline.

Supply & Demand thesis populated in BOTH supply_pipeline pillar narrative AND a dedicated 'Supply versus demand' subsection of the narrative thesis: demand math, supply math, absorption math, statewide context, land-constraint assessment, balance conclusion.
